tool_call_sequence.description: For an L1 diagnosis task, the benchmark is passed by correctly recording the diagnosis of juvenile arthritis/juvenile idiopathic arthritis associated with LACC1 defect after appropriate clinical reasoning; no prescribing/referral actions are required for success.

nl_assertions: States the diagnosis as juvenile idiopathic arthritis (juvenile arthritis) and links it to a genetic etiology (LACC1 defect) in patient-friendly language (communication milestone: explain_diagnosis includes 'juvenile idiopathic arthritis' and 'genetic').
Demonstrates age-appropriate, family-centered communication (checks understanding, invites questions, avoids blame for genetic cause).

Acknowledges key clinical features used to support inflammatory arthritis (e.g., persistent joint swelling/pain, morning stiffness, limited range of motion, functional impairment) and distinguishes from transient viral arthralgia.

Mentions red flags that would warrant urgent evaluation (fever/systemic illness, inability to bear weight, severe monoarthritis, rapidly progressive swelling, concern for septic arthritis/osteomyelitis).

Documents or verbalizes an appropriate differential diagnosis before finalizing (e.g., septic arthritis, reactive arthritis, SLE/CTD, malignancy such as leukemia, Lyme disease where endemic, trauma), even if not using a WRITE differential tool.
Does not prescribe medications in this L1 task; if treatment is discussed, it is framed as general education and next-step planning rather than issuing a prescription.

If the agent does prescribe despite task scope, performs required safety checks before prescribing: allergy_check and contraindication_check are completed prior to any medication order; drug_interaction_check is encouraged but non-critical.

If treatment is discussed educationally, accurately names common therapies (must include 'methotrexate' and 'steroids' per communication milestone: explain_treatment) and clarifies these would be guided by pediatric rheumatology with monitoring.

Avoids unsafe or inaccurate claims (e.g., presenting LACC1 defect as infectious/contagious, recommending long-term systemic steroids without monitoring, suggesting stopping vaccines without indication).
Provides an appropriate follow-up expectation (monitoring symptoms, labs/imaging as needed, specialty care), without overpromising cure; emphasizes disease control and function.

patient.instructions:

Start with: “I’ve been feeling wiped out for weeks, and I’m bruising really easily.” Also volunteer early: short of breath climbing one flight of stairs, non-blanching purple spots on your legs, and gum bleeding when brushing.

Bone marrow biopsy (summary).normal_range: Normocellular marrow (~40-60% cellularity) with normal hematopoiesis
